title: Hard Fork to Protocol Version 11 ('van Rossem' Hard Fork)

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this HardForkInitiation (active), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The action would upgrade Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to 11.0 while remaining in the Conway era." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data will improve performance and simplify script logic; Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says transaction shape is unchanged and ecosystem upgrade effort is minimized because this is an intra-era hard fork. Residual uncertainty: Protocol action lacks a complete independently pinned readiness packet: security_review_pass Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 50.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data will improve performance and simplify script logic.
Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says transaction shape is unchanged and ecosystem upgrade effort is minimized because this is an intra-era hard fork.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Initiate an intra-era Cardano mainnet hard fork to Protocol Version 11.0, named the van Rossem hard fork.
Stated amount: none requested from treasury

Deliverables: Upgrade Cardano mainnet protocol from major version 10 minor 0 to major version 11 minor 0., Keep the ledger in the Conway era with unchanged transaction shape., Enable new Plutus primitives from CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153., Make all Plutus built-in functions available consistently across Plutus V1, V2, and V3., Add UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data., Promote or tighten several ledger and node validation, diagnostic, and security rules.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The action would upgrade Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to 11.0 while remaining in the Conway era.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says the upgrade adds new Plutus primitives defined in CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says all Plutus built-ins will become available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3, expanding capabilities of existing V1 and V2 scripts.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data will improve performance and simplify script logic.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says transaction shape is unchanged and ecosystem upgrade effort is minimized because this is an intra-era hard fork.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete specification-implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus features.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says all Plutus built-ins will become available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3, expanding capabilities of existing V1 and V2 scripts.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data will improve performance and simplify script logic.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says transaction shape is unchanged and ecosystem upgrade effort is minimized because this is an intra-era hard fork.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says testing reports demonstrate no behavioral regressions, complete specification-implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus features.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The action would upgrade Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to 11.0 while remaining in the Conway era." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says all Plutus built-ins will become available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3, expanding capabilities of existing V1 and V2 scripts." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
